rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical Ru/Os-catalyzed C-H activation/annulation of benzoic acids with n-butyl acrylate to form isobenzofuranone (phthalide) products via Heck-type insertion and lactonization
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation regenerates the active high-valent Ru(II)/Os(II) catalyst (reoxidation of reduced metal species after C-H metalation/migratory insertion/β-H elimination); the metal carboxylate mediates C-H cleavage via CMD

key_intermediates.name: alkyl-metal intermediate after migratory insertion of acrylate

electron_flow: Metal carboxylate performs concerted metalation-deprotonation of the ortho C-H, then migratory insertion into n-butyl acrylate gives an alkyl-metal species which lactonizes to give the phthalide; the reduced metal is reoxidized at the anode (graphite felt) with H2 evolution at the Pt cathode.
rate_determining_step: C-H metalation is not rate-limiting (KIE ~1.1-1.2 from intermolecular competition and parallel experiments)

probe_reagents.name: D2O (H/D exchange on substrate IV)
probe_reagents.observation: Significant ortho-aryl H/D incorporation (8-10%D) on recovered benzoic acid IV and high D incorporation at benzylic/α positions of product 4 (47%D, 31%D, 11%D), indicating reversible C-H metalation

probe_reagents.name: Product 4 resubjected with D2O under electro-Ru catalysis
probe_reagents.observation: 0%D incorporation; [D]n-4 recovered 99% - product is not re-activated, ruling out post-catalysis exchange and confirming on-cycle C-H activation

probe_reagents.name: Product 4 resubjected with D2O under electro-Os catalysis
[D]7-I (perdeuterated substrate) intermolecular competition with I
probe_reagents.observation: KIE = 1.1, suggesting C-H cleavage is not rate-determining

probe_reagents.name: I vs [D]7-I parallel kinetic experiments
probe_reagents.observation: kH/kD = 0.3033/0.26 ≈ 1.2; consistent with non-rate-limiting C-H activation

probe_reagents.name: HR-ESI-MS detection of organometallic intermediates
probe_reagents.observation: Detected Os-p-cymene species bound to carboxylate and with cyclometalated naphthoate/alkyne adducts ([M+Na] = 519.0976, 725.2071, 761.2799, and lactone product at 399.1361), supporting metallacycle pathway

notes: Both Ru and Os catalysts probed in parallel and give analogous mechanistic behavior (reversible ortho C-H metalation, low KIE, no product re-exchange). High-resolution MS identifies plausible metallacyclic intermediates including carboxylate-bound and alkyne/alkenyl-inserted species (drawn for a related naphthoic acid/alkyne system in Fig S12). Electrochemistry (CCE 4.0 mA, GF anode / Pt cathode) serves to reoxidize the metal rather than to generate substrate-centered radicals, consistent with inner-sphere electrocatalysis.
